body:
### Problem

Currently, [`resolve_lang`](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/a8361c3afc5b9281814e9f16a9d4291e095b38fa/runtime/lua/vim/treesitter/languagetree.lua#L1025-L1028) validates injection language names against the pattern `[%w_]+`, which rejects any name containing a hyphen.

This silently drops injections for a large number of language identifiers that are in widespread use. For example, in a Markdown fenced code block:

````
```shell-session
$ go build ./...
```
````

Even with `vim.treesitter.language.register('bash', 'shell-session')` configured, the injection never fires because `resolve_lang` returns nil before ever consulting the registration table.

Hyphenated identifiers are standard across major tools:

- [Prism.js](https://github.com/PrismJS/prism/tree/master/components): shell-session, css-extras, go-module, firestore-security-rules, web-idl, visual-basic, etc.
- [Shiki](https://shiki.style/languages): actionscript-3, common-lisp, emacs-lisp, git-commit, git-rebase, objective-c, ssh-config, system-verilog, etc.
- [GitHub Linguist](https://github.com/github-linguist/linguist/blob/main/lib/linguist/languages.yml): Objective-C, CoNLL-U, ssh-config, Tree-sitter Query, etc.

The fix would be to either:
1. Widen the pattern to allow hyphens: `[%w_-]+`
2. Normalize hyphens to underscores before validation and lookup, so shell-session resolves the same as shell_session

Option 2 would also align with how vim.filetype handles extension matching.

Relates to #27080.

Happy to open a PR for this, just wanted to get feedback on if this is feasible / which approach would be preferred.

### Steps to reproduce

```
vim.treesitter.language.register('bash', 'shell-session')
```

...

open markdown file with:

````
```shell-session
$ go build ./...
```
````

### Expected behavior

I expect `shell-session` to get `bash` injected syntax highlighting.

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

0.11.6

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

n/a

### Operating system/version

macOS 26.3

### Terminal name/version

ghostty 1.2.3

### $TERM environment variable

xterm-ghostty

### Installation

homebrew
title: treesitter: resolve_lang rejects hyphenated language names
